root_motivation: The Gen-9 hypothesis engine produces a ranked HypothesisSet with a concrete probe MQO per hypothesis but deliberately stops at proposing — no code consumes those MQOs because the budget governor didn't exist yet. Now that mcp-query-budget-governor ships a check()/ingest_audit_log() verdict, the single missing piece is an executor that calls check() before each probe, fires the MQO via the proven replayer stdio JSON-RPC client on Proceed/CheckIn, decides confirm/refute from the observed component delta vs. predicted direction, halts cleanly on Halt, and returns a ResolvedHypothesisSet. Without this, the budget governor is a leash no one holds and every WatchEvent stays permanently unresolved.
user_persona: An automated investigation pipeline (mcp-investigation-loop) running on a developer's macOS laptop as part of the Gen-10 closed-loop; may also be invoked directly in CI with --summaries flag. No human in the hot path during normal execution.

five_whys_trace.q: Why does mcp-probe-executor need to exist at all?
five_whys_trace.a: The hypothesis engine emits probe MQOs but nothing executes them — the loop is open and every WatchEvent stays unresolved forever.

five_whys_trace.q: Why can't mcp-investigation-loop just call the MQO client directly?
five_whys_trace.a: Budget gating must happen per-probe, not once up front; the executor is the only place that interleaves check() before each MQO and folds latency_ms back into ingest_audit_log() — collapsing it into the orchestrator would make that contract untestable in isolation.

five_whys_trace.q: Why must budget gating be testable in isolation?
five_whys_trace.a: A Halt verdict mid-flight must record partial results correctly (some hypotheses resolved, some not), and that invariant is impossible to assert if the executor and orchestrator are the same binary.

acceptance_criteria.description: Reads a HypothesisSet JSON from --input (or stdin), calls mcp-query-budget-governor::check() before each probe, and emits a ResolvedHypothesisSet JSON to --output (or stdout) with every hypothesis marked confirm or refute.
acceptance_criteria.test: tests/acceptance_ac1.rs — feeds a 3-hypothesis set with a stub governor returning Proceed for all; asserts all three hypotheses appear in output with confirm/refute set.

acceptance_criteria.description: Stops execution on a Halt verdict, writes a partial ResolvedHypothesisSet containing results for already-executed probes, exits with code 2.
acceptance_criteria.test: tests/acceptance_ac2.rs — stub governor returns Proceed, Proceed, Halt; asserts output contains exactly 2 resolved hypotheses and exit code is 2.

acceptance_criteria.description: Calls ingest_audit_log() with actual latency_ms after each successful probe execution so the governor's spend tracking is updated.
acceptance_criteria.test: tests/acceptance_ac3.rs — spy stub governor records ingest_audit_log calls; asserts one call per executed probe with latency_ms > 0.

acceptance_criteria.description: In --summaries mode (no live MCP server), executes against pre-baked DatasetSummary JSON fixtures and produces deterministic confirm/refute decisions.
acceptance_criteria.test: tests/acceptance_ac4.rs — runs executor with --summaries pointing to fixture dir; asserts output is byte-stable across two runs.

acceptance_criteria.description: Confirm/refute decision compares the probe's observed component delta to the hypothesis's predicted direction; a delta in the predicted direction is confirm, opposite or negligible is refute.
acceptance_criteria.test: tests/acceptance_ac5.rs — two hypotheses with opposing predicted directions, probe returns same delta for both; asserts one confirm and one refute.

acceptance_criteria.description: On a CheckIn verdict, logs a structured warning (to stderr) before continuing, so the orchestrator can detect budget pressure without stopping the probe loop.
acceptance_criteria.test: tests/acceptance_ac6.rs — stub governor returns CheckIn; asserts stderr contains 'budget_check_in' JSON key and execution continues.

acceptance_criteria.description: Handles malformed or missing probe_mqo fields in a hypothesis gracefully — skips the probe, marks the hypothesis as refute with reason 'malformed_probe', and continues.
acceptance_criteria.test: tests/acceptance_ac7.rs — input has one hypothesis with null probe_mqo; asserts it appears in output as refute with reason 'malformed_probe' and remaining hypotheses are unaffected.

acceptance_criteria.description: Accepts --concurrency N to execute up to N probes in parallel (each still individually gated by check() before launch).
acceptance_criteria.test: tests/acceptance_ac8.rs — 5 hypotheses with --concurrency 2; asserts all 5 resolved and total wall-time < sequential lower bound (stub governor latency * 5).

scope: Accept a HypothesisSet JSON (from mcp-hypothesis-engine) and BudgetLimits on the command line or via pipe.

For each hypothesis in ranked order: call mcp-query-budget-governor::check(); on Proceed or CheckIn execute the hypothesis's probe_mqo via the replayer stdio JSON-RPC client; fold latency_ms into ingest_audit_log(); decide confirm or refute by comparing observed component delta to predicted direction.
On Halt verdict: stop the loop, record partial results for already-executed probes, exit with code 2.
Emit a ResolvedHypothesisSet JSON (same hypotheses with confirm/refute + observed delta attached).
Support --summaries mode for CI: load DatasetSummary fixtures from disk instead of making live MQO calls.
Reuse the replayer stdio JSON-RPC client shape already proven in mcp-investigation-replayer and mcp-watch-daemon.
non_goals: Does not generate or rank hypotheses — that is mcp-hypothesis-engine's responsibility.
Does not persist findings to any store — that is mcp-finding-store's responsibility; this crate returns a ResolvedHypothesisSet and the caller decides what to do with it.
Does not orchestrate the full WatchEvent → hypothesis → probe → finding pipeline — that is mcp-investigation-loop.
Does not implement the budget governor logic itself — only consumes mcp-query-budget-governor::check() and ingest_audit_log() as a path dependency.
Does not serve an HTTP or MCP endpoint — pure library + thin CLI binary only.
Does not auto-apply any tuning or threshold changes — read-only with respect to all standing-query and model stores.
Does not support streaming re-evaluation against a live change-feed — batch probe execution only.

additional.intra_gen_path_dep: mcp-query-budget-governor as a path dependency (intra-Gen-10); compose-by-JSON at gen boundary with mcp-hypothesis-engine
additional.pure_now_ms: no SystemTime::now() calls inside library functions; caller supplies now_ms for all budget check invocations
additional.no_serving_substrate: no tokio-based server, no axum, no tonic; the binary is a thin CLI wrapper over the lib

ambiguities_resolved.question: Vision lists mcp-probe-executor as 'rust-cli' but the task specifies target=lib. Which wins?
ambiguities_resolved.resolution: Task context wins: target_kind=lib with a thin CLI binary in src/main.rs that calls into the library. The vision's 'rust-cli' label describes the final artifact shape; the autobuilder target_kind describes the primary crate type, which is lib so the executor logic is importable by mcp-investigation-loop as a path dep.

ambiguities_resolved.question: Should confirm/refute use a numeric threshold on delta magnitude or purely sign-comparison?
ambiguities_resolved.resolution: Sign-comparison by default (predicted direction matches observed direction = confirm); a negligible delta (|delta| < epsilon, default 0.0) is refute. Epsilon is a BudgetLimits-adjacent config field, not hard-coded, so callers can tune it without recompiling.
